title: Green Rice Porridge
description: A warm rice pudding made with pandan and coconut milk, finished with a palm sugar coconut syrup.
ingredients: Coconut syrup: 3 medium-sized pieces of palm sugar
steps: Rice Pudding: In a pot, add water, coconut milk, salt, pandan leaves, and rice flour. Stir well until dissolved. Cook while continuously stirring until it thickens and bubbles. Turn off heat and set aside.
Coconut Syrup: In a pot, cook all the coconut syrup ingredients over low heat until it thickens, stirring occasionally to prevent burning.
To Serve: Spoon the rice pudding into bowls. Drizzle the coconut syrup over the top. Serve warm or chilled.

title: Scrambled Tofu
description: A savory, spiced tofu scramble with kale, bell pepper, and a squeeze of lemon.
ingredients: 1/2 cup chopped bell pepper (mix of green and red)
1 tbsp water (optional, if mixture looks too dry)
steps: Prep tofu: Crumble tofu and set aside.
Sauté veggies: Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Sauté red onion, garlic, and green onion for 2 minutes. Add tomato and cook 2 more minutes. Add bell pepper and cook 1 minute.
Add spices: Stir in turmeric, red chili powder, cumin, smoked paprika, and salt.
Cook tofu: Add crumbled tofu and mash with a potato masher to combine with veggies and spices. Add water if needed. Stir in kale and cook 1 minute.
Finish and serve: Turn off heat, cover, let sit 2 minutes. Stir in lemon juice and garnish with cilantro. Sprinkle nutritional yeast if desired.

title: Granola
description: A big-batch toasted granola with coconut, pumpkin and sunflower seeds, cashews, and dried fruit.
ingredients: Simple syrup: 250 g sugar + 60 ml water
steps: Preheat oven to 150°C (300°F).
Prepare simple syrup: combine sugar and water in a small saucepan, heat until sugar dissolves. Set aside.
Mix dry ingredients: combine oats, coconut flakes, seeds, cashews, salt, and cinnamon in a large bowl.
Add syrup: pour over the dry mixture and stir until coated.
Bake: spread on a lined baking sheet. Bake 25-30 minutes, stirring every 10 minutes.
Add fruits: once golden, stir in raisins and dates while still warm.
Cool & store in an airtight container.

title: Vegan Gluten Free Coconut Bread
description: A moist rice-and-tapioca-flour coconut bread, naturally gluten-free.
steps: Preheat oven to 160-180°C (320-356°F).
Combine rice flour, tapioca flour, sugar, and desiccated coconut. Add coconut milk, water, and salt. Add vanilla if using. Mix into a smooth batter.
Pour into a greased or lined baking pan. Bake 40-50 minutes, until a toothpick comes out clean.
Cool in the pan 10 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack before slicing.
Tip: lightly toast the shredded coconut first for extra flavor.

title: Vegan Gluten Free Banana Bread
description: A rice-and-tapioca-flour banana bread, soft and naturally gluten-free.
ingredients: 1/2 cup water or any milk (dairy or non-dairy)
steps: Preheat oven to 160-180°C (320-356°F).
Combine mashed banana, rice flour, tapioca flour, coconut palm sugar, melted coconut oil, and water or milk. Add cinnamon and baking powder. Mix until smooth.
Pour into a greased or lined loaf pan. Bake 40-50 minutes, until a toothpick comes out clean.
Cool in the pan 10 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack before slicing.

title: Jamu
description: A traditional Indonesian turmeric-ginger tonic, warm or chilled.
ingredients: 2-3 cm fresh turmeric, thinly sliced (or 1-2 tsp turmeric powder)
1-2 tbsp maple syrup (adjust to taste, in place of honey)
Optional additions: lemongrass, bruised galangal, black pepper, crushed cloves, cinnamon
steps: Bring water to a boil. Add turmeric, ginger, and any optional herbs/spices. Lower heat and simmer 15-20 minutes to infuse.
Strain the liquid to remove solids. Stir in maple syrup and lime or lemon juice. Adjust sweetness to taste.
Serve warm or chilled.

title: Peanut Butter
description: Simple homemade roasted peanut butter, smooth and creamy.
steps: Place roasted peanuts in a food processor or chopper.
Blend until they break down into a coarse mixture, then a paste, then a smooth, creamy butter — this may take several minutes.
While blending, gradually add coconut oil to reach desired consistency. Add salt to taste.
Transfer to an airtight container and store in the refrigerator.

title: Tempeh Tandoori Skewers
description: Marinated, grilled tempeh skewers in a coconut-spice tandoori marinade.
steps: Prepare marinade: blend coconut milk, garlic, ginger, garam masala, cumin, coriander, smoked paprika, black pepper, sea salt, lemon juice, and mustard until smooth.
Marinate tempeh: cut tempeh into small pieces and add to the marinade for at least 30 minutes. Steam marinated tempeh 5 minutes, trim, and cut into small batons.
Prepare skewers: soak bamboo skewers in water 15 minutes. Thread tempeh onto skewers.
Grill: heat a char grill pan, lightly spray with oil. Cook skewers on each side until nicely charred.

title: Baked Sweet Potato with Sweet Paprika and Thyme
description: Roasted sweet potato wedges with paprika and thyme.
ingredients: 2 large sweet potatoes – peeled (optional) and cut into wedges or cubes
1/2 tsp dried thyme (or 1 tsp fresh thyme leaves)
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Line a baking tray with parchment paper.
Place sweet potato wedges or cubes in a large bowl.
Add olive oil, sweet paprika, thyme, salt (and pepper if using). Toss to coat evenly.
Spread in a single layer on the tray. Roast 25-30 minutes, turning halfway, until golden and tender.
Serve hot as a side dish or snack.

title: Avocado Salad with Parsley
description: A fresh, simple salad of avocado, tomato, cucumber, and parsley in a lime dressing.
ingredients: 1 cup cherry tomatoes or 2 medium tomatoes – diced
steps: In a medium bowl, combine avocado, tomato, cucumber, and parsley.
In a small bowl, whisk olive oil, lime juice, salt, and black pepper.
Pour dressing over the salad and gently toss to coat.
Serve immediately for the freshest flavor.

title: Three-Color Salad (Rucola, Beetroot, White Cabbage)
description: A vibrant, cleansing salad of arugula, beetroot, and shredded cabbage with a lime dressing.
ingredients: 1 small beetroot, cooked or roasted, thinly sliced or julienned
Optional garnish: toasted nuts (walnut, almond, or sunflower seed)
steps: Boil or roast beetroot until tender, cool, and slice thinly. Shred white cabbage finely. Wash and dry rucola leaves.
Whisk together olive oil, lime juice, salt, and black pepper.
Arrange rucola, cabbage, and beetroot in a serving bowl or plate. Drizzle with dressing.
Toss gently just before serving. Enjoy fresh as a side dish.

title: Waldorf Salad (Vegan Version)
description: A creamy vegan mayo-dressed salad of romaine, apple, celery, and raisins.
ingredients: 1 small head baby romaine, roughly chopped
1 tsp lemon juice or apple cider vinegar
Salt and black pepper, to taste
Optional garnish: crushed walnuts or pumpkin seeds, fresh parsley, a few grapes
steps: Mix vegan mayo, lemon juice, maple syrup, salt, and black pepper. Whisk until smooth.
In a large bowl, combine romaine, green tomato, apple, celery, and raisins.
Gently fold in the dressing until evenly coated.
Chill 10-15 minutes if desired, then serve fresh as a light lunch or side dish.

title: Vegan Mayonnaise (Soy Milk Base)
description: A simple emulsified soy-milk mayonnaise you can make at home.
ingredients: 1 cup neutral oil (sunflower, canola, or light olive oil)
1 1/2 tsp apple cider vinegar or lemon juice
Optional: a pinch of garlic powder or maple syrup
steps: Add soy milk, vinegar (or lemon juice), mustard, and salt to a tall container or blender jar.
Start blending on medium speed. Slowly drizzle in the oil in a thin, steady stream while blending continuously until thick and creamy (1-2 minutes).
Adjust texture: if too thin, blend longer or add more oil; if too thick, add 1-2 tsp soy milk. Taste and adjust seasoning.
Transfer to a clean jar and refrigerate. Keeps up to 7 days.

title: Protein Salad with Tahini Dressing
description: A hearty mixed-bean salad with soaked seeds and a creamy tahini-lemon dressing.
ingredients: 1 cup mixed beans (chickpeas, red beans, black beans – cooked or canned)
1 small head baby romaine – chopped or torn
Optional: cucumber, fresh mint or parsley
1 tsp maple syrup (in place of honey)
steps: Whisk tahini, lemon juice, garlic, salt, and pepper. Add water gradually until smooth and creamy.
In a large bowl, combine beans, soaked seeds, cherry tomatoes, and romaine.
Pour the tahini dressing over and toss gently to coat.
Garnish with extra seeds or chopped herbs. Enjoy chilled or at room temperature.

title: Chayote–Mango Salad with Raisins & Parsley
description: A refreshing salad of julienned chayote and mango in a cumin-lime dressing.
ingredients: 1 medium chayote squash – peeled, seeded, julienned or thinly sliced
1 small ripe but firm mango – peeled, julienned or diced
steps: Peel, halve, and seed chayote; julienne or slice thinly. Blanch 1-2 minutes if a softer texture is preferred, then drain and cool.
Combine chayote, mango, and parsley in a bowl.
Whisk lime juice, olive oil (if using), cumin, coriander, sweet paprika, salt, and pepper until smooth.
Pour dressing over the salad mixture and toss gently to coat.
Taste and adjust; chill or serve immediately.

title: Butternut Squash Salad with Maple Dressing
description: Roasted butternut squash over arugula and kale with pomegranate and a maple-Dijon dressing.
ingredients: 2 cups butternut squash – peeled and cubed (about 1.5 cm pieces)
Optional: roasted walnuts or pumpkin seeds
1 tbsp apple cider vinegar or lemon juice
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Toss cubed butternut with olive oil, salt, and pepper. Roast 25-30 minutes until golden and tender. Cool slightly.
Massage chopped kale gently with a little olive oil and salt for 1 minute to soften. Add rucola and toss.
Whisk dressing ingredients until smooth.
Add roasted butternut, pomegranate seeds, and any nuts/seeds to the greens. Drizzle with maple dressing and toss.
Serve immediately or chill briefly.

title: Syrian Salad (Romaine Salad with Za'atar Dressing)
description: A crisp romaine salad with tomato, cucumber, mint, and a za'atar-olive oil dressing.
ingredients: 1/4 cup minced fresh mint or 1 tbsp dried mint
steps: Place lettuce, tomato, cucumber, onion, parsley, and mint in a large salad bowl.
Squeeze lemon juice over the bowl. Grate garlic directly over the salad.
Drizzle with olive oil and sprinkle with za'atar. Season with salt and pepper.
Gently toss and check seasoning before serving.

title: Brown Lentil Salad
description: A simple lentil salad with cherry tomatoes, parsley, and a cumin-lime dressing.
ingredients: 1 1/2 cups cooked brown lentils (or 1 can, drained & rinsed)
steps: If using dried lentils, cook until just tender, drain, and let cool.
In a bowl, mix lentils, cherry tomatoes, and parsley.
Whisk olive oil, lime juice, cumin, salt, and black pepper.
Pour dressing over the lentil mixture and toss to coat.
Serve chilled or at room temperature.

title: Olivier Salad (Russian Potato Salad)
description: A classic potato salad with carrots, peas, and pickles in a mustard-mayo dressing.
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
1 tbsp lemon juice or pickle brine
Garnish: fresh dill or parsley – finely chopped
steps: Boil potatoes, carrots, and peas separately until tender (don't overcook). Drain and cool completely.
In a large bowl, combine cooled potatoes, carrots, peas, and diced pickles.
Whisk vegan mayo, mustard, lemon juice (or brine), salt, and pepper.
Pour dressing over the salad and mix gently until well coated.
Refrigerate at least 30 minutes before serving. Garnish with dill or parsley.

title: Steamed Chayote & Carrot with Peanut Sauce
description: Steamed vegetables with a traditional pounded peanut-tamarind sauce.
ingredients: 1 medium chayote – peeled and sliced into sticks
1 medium carrot – peeled and sliced into sticks
Sauce: 1/2 cup roasted peanuts (or 3 tbsp natural peanut butter)
1 tbsp tamarind water (or 1 tsp lime juice)
steps: Steam chayote and carrots (and green beans if using) for 5-7 minutes until tender but still bright. Set aside.

Grind roasted peanuts, garlic, and chilies in a mortar & pestle or food processor. Add palm sugar, salt, and tamarind water. Slowly add warm water until smooth and slightly thick. (If using peanut butter, simply mix all ingredients until creamy.)
Arrange steamed vegetables on a plate, spoon peanut sauce generously on top. Serve warm or at room temperature.

title: Moringa Soup
description: A light, brothy soup with moringa leaves, sweet corn, and basil.
steps: Boil water with shallot and corn until corn is tender. Season with salt and mushroom powder.
Reduce heat, add moringa and sweet basil leaves.
Simmer briefly and serve hot.

title: Beetroot Soup
description: A smooth, vibrant puréed beetroot soup brightened with lemon.
ingredients: Optional garnish: fresh parsley, a swirl of coconut cream
steps: Heat olive oil in a pot over medium heat. Add onion (and garlic, if using) and sauté until soft.
Stir in diced beetroot and cook 2-3 minutes.
Pour in broth or water. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer 20-25 minutes until beetroot is tender.
Blend until smooth using an immersion or regular blender (careful with hot soup).
Season with salt, pepper, and lemon juice/vinegar to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with parsley or a swirl of coconut cream.

title: Red Bean Vegetable Stew
description: A hearty stew of red beans, sweet potato, and carrot in a tomato base.
steps: Soak red beans in water overnight or at least 8 hours. Drain and rinse.
Dice onion, carrot, and sweet potato.
Cook the beans: cover with water, bring to a boil, then simmer 1-1.5 hours until tender. Drain and set aside.
In the same pot, heat a little oil and sauté onion, carrot, and sweet potato about 10 minutes until softening.
Stir in tomato paste and paprika, cook 2 minutes.
Add cooked beans back in with enough water to cover (about 4 cups). Season with salt and pepper. Bring to a boil, then simmer about 30 minutes until vegetables are tender and flavors meld.

title: Kitchari
description: A classic Ayurvedic one-pot rice and lentil dish, gently spiced with cumin and turmeric.
ingredients: Optional spices for masala khichdi: 1/2 tsp red chili powder, 1/2 tsp coriander powder, 1/2 tsp garam masala powder, 1 tsp grated ginger
steps: Heat a deep pot over medium heat. Add coconut oil and cumin seeds; let them splutter.
Add chopped onions and veggies (if using). Sauté a couple minutes until softened. Add turmeric, salt, and optional spices if making masala khichdi.
Rinse rice and lentils thoroughly, add to the pot with 4 cups water and stir well.
Cook over medium heat until lentils and rice reach desired consistency, adjusting water as needed.
Turn off heat, garnish with cilantro. Serve hot.

title: Black Bean Stew with Sweet Potato
description: A warming black bean and sweet potato stew, spiced with cumin and coriander.
ingredients: 2 cups cooked black beans (or 1 can, drained & rinsed)
1 medium sweet potato – peeled and diced
1-2 medium tomatoes – chopped (or 1 cup crushed tomatoes)
Optional garnish: fresh cilantro or parsley, lime wedges
steps: Heat olive oil in a large pot. Add onion and cook until soft. Add garlic and cook 1 minute.
Stir in sweet paprika, cumin, and coriander; cook 30 seconds.
Mix in sweet potato, bell pepper, and tomato.
Add black beans and vegetable broth. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer uncovered 20-25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until sweet potato is tender and stew thickens.
Season with salt and pepper. Serve hot, garnished with cilantro/parsley or lime.

title: Cannellini and Vegetable Stew
description: A comforting Italian-style white bean stew with carrot, celery, and zucchini.
ingredients: 2 cups cooked cannellini beans (or 1 can, drained & rinsed)
2 medium tomatoes – chopped (or 1 cup crushed tomatoes)
Fresh parsley or basil for garnish
steps: Heat olive oil in a large pot. Add onion, cook until translucent. Add garlic, cook 1 minute.
Stir in carrot, celery, zucchini, and bell pepper. Cook 4-5 minutes until slightly softened.
Add chopped tomatoes, paprika, thyme/oregano, salt, and pepper. Cook 2-3 minutes until tomatoes break down.
Stir in cannellini beans and vegetable broth. Bring to a boil.
Lower heat and simmer 15-20 minutes until vegetables are tender and stew thickens slightly.
Adjust seasoning and serve hot, garnished with parsley or basil.

title: Snow Fungus "Yin Er" Soup
description: A delicate Chinese-style soup with snow fungus, carrot, and leek in a light broth.
ingredients: 20 g dried snow fungus (Yin Er) – soaked, trimmed, and cut into pieces
1 tbsp olive oil or neutral oil
steps: Soak dried snow fungus 20-30 minutes until fully expanded. Rinse, remove the tough base, and cut into bite-size pieces.
Heat oil in a pot over medium heat. Add garlic and leek, sauté 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Stir in diced carrot and snow fungus, cook another 2 minutes.
Pour in broth or water. Bring to a boil, then simmer 10-15 minutes until carrots are tender.
Season with salt and black pepper.
Stir the cornstarch slurry, then slowly pour into the soup while stirring until slightly thickened. Simmer 1-2 minutes.
Serve hot, garnished with extra leek or parsley if desired.

title: Vegan Tikka Masala
description: Crispy pan-seared tofu in a creamy, spiced tomato-coconut curry sauce.
ingredients: 2 (14-ounce) blocks firm or extra firm tofu, pressed and cubed
10 sprigs fresh cilantro (plus more for serving)
7.5 oz can full-fat coconut cream or milk
Optional for serving: chopped cilantro, extra yogurt, basmati rice
steps: Prepare sauce base: in a food processor, combine half the onion, garlic, ginger, cilantro stalks, and 1 tbsp olive oil. Blitz into a smooth paste; set aside.
Prepare tofu: heat remaining oil in a large skillet, add cubed tofu, sauté until lightly browned and crispy on all sides, about 15 minutes. Set tofu aside.
Prepare sauce: in the same skillet, heat oil, add finely chopped onion, cook about 8 minutes until softened and lightly browned.
Add cumin, turmeric, cinnamon, paprika, salt, and garam masala; cook 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in tomato sauce, vegan yogurt, and coconut cream. Add the blitzed onion-cilantro paste. Stir well and simmer about 5 minutes.
Add the sautéed tofu and frozen peas. Stir gently and cook an additional 5 minutes until peas are heated through.
Serve over basmati rice, garnished with cilantro and a dollop of vegan yogurt.

title: Spinach Curry with Chickpeas
description: A creamy coconut-milk spinach and chickpea curry, warmly spiced.
ingredients: 3 cups fresh spinach leaves – roughly chopped
1/2 cup coconut milk (or water for lighter version)
steps: Heat coconut oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds; let sizzle a few seconds. Add onion and sauté until soft and golden.
Stir in garlic, ginger, and chili. Cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Mix in turmeric, coriander, chili powder, and garam masala. Add diced tomatoes and cook until soft and pulpy.
Stir in chickpeas and a splash of water. Simmer 5-7 minutes so they absorb flavor.
Add spinach, cook until wilted. Pour in coconut milk, stir well, and simmer 3-5 minutes until creamy.
Season with salt. Garnish with fresh coriander and a drizzle of coconut milk or plant-based yogurt if desired.

title: Creamy Black Bean
description: A quick, coconut-milk-enriched black bean side dish or dip.
ingredients: 2 cups cooked black beans (or 1 can, drained & rinsed)
1 tbsp olive oil or coconut oil
1/2 tsp dried thyme (or 1 tsp fresh thyme leaves)
1/2-1 cup vegetable broth or water (adjust for thickness)
steps: Heat oil in a pot over medium heat. Add garlic and cook 1-2 minutes until fragrant but not browned.
Stir in black beans and thyme; cook another minute.
Pour in coconut milk and a bit of vegetable broth/water. Stir well.
Season with salt (and black pepper if using) to taste.
For a creamier texture, mash some beans with the back of a spoon or blend half, then return to the pot.
Serve hot as a side dish, dip, or over rice.

title: Red Bean Balls with Red Sauce
description: Baked spiced red bean balls in a creamy tomato-coconut white sauce.
ingredients: Red Bean Balls: 1 kg red beans, boiled
Salt and pepper to taste, coconut milk, water, nutritional yeast
steps: Red Bean Balls: pulse boiled red beans in a food processor into small chunks without pureeing. Transfer to a mixing bowl.
Finely chop onion, garlic in the food processor; combine with the red beans.
Mix in cumin, coriander, garam masala, curry powder, salt, and black pepper. Chill the mixture at least an hour.
Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Shape mixture into balls and place on a baking sheet. Bake 20 minutes, flip, bake another 20 minutes until golden brown.
White Sauce: sauté chopped garlic until golden. Add crushed tomatoes, oregano, basil, coconut milk, and water. Bring to a boil, then simmer until creamy. Stir in salt and nutritional yeast to taste.

title: Jackfruit BBQ with Tamarind & Coconut Aminos
description: Shredded young jackfruit simmered in a tangy tamarind-coconut aminos BBQ sauce.
ingredients: 2 cans (400 g each) young green jackfruit in brine – drained, rinsed, and shredded
Salt and black pepper to taste
Fresh coriander or parsley for garnish (optional)
steps: Drain and rinse jackfruit well. Pat dry and shred into pieces with fingers or forks, removing seeds if desired.
Heat coconut oil in a large skillet over medium heat, add onion, sauté until translucent, about 4-5 minutes. Stir in garlic, paprika, cooking 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in tomato paste, coconut aminos, tamarind paste, and coconut sugar. Add water and whisk into a smooth sauce; simmer 2 minutes.
Add shredded jackfruit, stirring to coat. Lower heat, cover, and cook gently 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until tender and sauce thickens.
Adjust seasoning with salt, pepper, tamarind, or coconut sugar. Serve warm in tacos, on buns, or with steamed rice.

title: Creamy Tempeh
description: Golden pan-seared tempeh simmered in a coconut milk and thyme sauce.
ingredients: 250 g tempeh – cut into bite-size cubes or slices
1 tbsp olive oil or coconut oil
1/2 tsp dried thyme (or 1 tsp fresh thyme leaves)
1/2 cup vegetable broth or water (adjust for thickness)
steps: Steam tempeh 10 minutes (optional, reduces bitterness), then pat dry.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add tempeh, cook until lightly golden on all sides, about 4-5 minutes. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, add garlic and sauté 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in coconut milk and vegetable broth. Stir in thyme, salt, and black pepper. Simmer gently 2-3 minutes.
Return tempeh to the pan. Simmer another 5 minutes, letting the sauce thicken and coat the tempeh.
Adjust salt, add a squeeze of lime or lemon if desired. Serve hot with rice, quinoa, or bread.

title: Fajita Vegetables
description: A spiced sauté of cauliflower, broccoli, and mushroom, fajita-style.
ingredients: Optional garnish: lime wedges, fresh cilantro
steps: Cut cauliflower and broccoli into small florets. Slice mushrooms and onion.
Combine cumin, coriander, paprika, nutmeg, salt, and black pepper in a small bowl.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add onion and garlic, sauté 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add cauliflower and broccoli first, cook 4-5 minutes, stirring often. Then add mushrooms.
Sprinkle the spice mix over the veggies and toss well. Cook another 3-4 minutes until tender but still a bit crisp.
Adjust seasoning. Serve hot with rice or as a side dish; squeeze lime on top if desired.

title: Grilled Asparagus & Mushrooms with Garlic and Fresh Herbs
description: Simple grilled asparagus and mushrooms tossed with garlic and fresh herbs.
ingredients: 150 g button mushrooms – halved or thickly sliced
3 garlic cloves – minced (or 1 tsp garlic powder)
2 tbsp fresh herbs – chopped (parsley, thyme, rosemary, or a mix)
Optional: a squeeze of lemon juice for serving
steps: Trim woody ends from asparagus. Clean mushrooms and cut into halves or thick slices.
Mix olive oil, minced garlic, salt, black pepper, and chopped herbs in a bowl. Toss asparagus and mushrooms in this mixture.

Grill on a grill pan or outdoor grill preheated to medium-high, 3-4 minutes per side for asparagus and 4-5 minutes per side for mushrooms, until tender with light char. (Or broil in the oven 5-8 minutes, flipping once.)
Serve hot, drizzled with extra olive oil or a squeeze of lemon juice if desired.

title: Sautéed Broccoli & Mushroom
description: A quick garlicky broccoli and mushroom stir-fry with a touch of sesame.
ingredients: 1 small head broccoli – cut into florets
Squeeze of lemon juice or dash of vinegar (optional)
steps: Blanch broccoli florets in boiling water 1 minute, then drain and rinse with cold water to keep them crisp and green.
Heat olive oil in a large pan or wok. Add garlic (and onion if using); sauté until fragrant.
Add sliced mushrooms and cook 3-4 minutes until they release moisture and start to brown.
Add the broccoli and toss until coated. Pour in tamari, add black pepper, and finish with sesame oil if desired. Stir-fry 2-3 minutes until broccoli is warmed through but still bright and crisp.
Adjust seasoning and serve.

title: Sautéed Water Spinach (Lombok-Style Spicy Spinach Salad)
description: Blanched water spinach topped with a pounded chili-tomato sambal.
ingredients: 1 bunch water spinach (kangkung) – washed and cut into 10 cm pieces
3 red big chilies (or more to taste)
1 tbsp coconut oil or vegetable oil
steps: Bring a pot of water to a boil with a pinch of salt. Add water spinach and blanch about 30 seconds until wilted but still bright green. Drain and rinse with cold water to keep crisp.

Heat oil in a small pan and sauté shallots, garlic, chilies, and tomatoes until soft and slightly charred. Transfer to a mortar & pestle (or blender). Add salt and mushroom powder; pound or blend into a coarse paste.
Stir lime juice into the sambal.
Arrange the blanched water spinach on a plate, spoon the sambal generously over the top. Serve warm or at room temperature.

title: Zucchini Mufarakeh (Middle Eastern Sautéed Zucchini with Mint)
description: Golden sautéed zucchini finished with fresh and dried mint.
ingredients: 2 medium zucchini, cut into small cubes (1-2 cm)
1 tbsp olive oil (plus extra if needed)
1/2 cup Protena (soy protein) or tofu, optional – cooked or lightly pan-grilled
steps: Heat a grill pan or skillet over medium heat. Drizzle with olive oil, then grill or sauté the zucchini cubes until lightly golden and tender, about 5-7 minutes.
In the same pan, sauté chopped onion until soft and translucent, then add minced garlic and cook about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the cooked zucchini and Protena or tofu. Season with salt and black pepper, stir well, and cook 2-3 minutes to combine flavors.
Turn off heat. Sprinkle with dried mint and fresh mint leaves, drizzle a little extra olive oil if desired.
Serve warm or at room temperature.

title: Vegetable Sambar
description: A South Indian lentil and vegetable stew, tempered with mustard seeds and fenugreek.
ingredients: 1/2 cup mung beans (yellow mung bean) – rinsed
1/2 cup green beans, cut into 3-4 cm pieces
1/2 tsp paprika powder (or mild chili powder)
steps: Cook mung beans in a pot with 2 1/2 cups water; bring to a boil and simmer until soft, about 20-25 minutes. Lightly mash and set aside.
Heat coconut oil in a deep pot, add mustard seeds and let them pop. Add fenugreek seeds and sauté briefly. Add onion and ginger, cook until onion turns soft and golden.
Add chopped tomato with coriander, cumin, turmeric, and paprika. Stir and cook until tomato softens into a paste.
Add carrot, green beans, pumpkin, and daikon; pour in 1 1/2-2 cups water. Simmer 10-12 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Add the cooked mung beans, stir well, and simmer another 5-10 minutes to blend flavors. Adjust salt and thickness as needed.
Finish with a drizzle of coconut oil. Sprinkle with fresh coriander and serve hot with steamed rice.

title: Tofu Barbeque
description: Pan-fried or baked tofu glazed in a tangy homemade BBQ sauce.
ingredients: 250 g firm tofu, pressed and cut into cubes or slices
BBQ Sauce: 3 tbsp tomato sauce (or ketchup)
steps: Pat tofu dry, season lightly with salt and pepper. Pan-fry in a little oil 3-4 minutes per side, or bake at 180°C (350°F) for 20 minutes, flipping halfway, until golden.
Make the BBQ sauce: in a small pan, combine tomato sauce, vinegar, brown sugar, garlic, and water. Simmer over low heat 3-5 minutes until slightly thickened. Season with salt and pepper.
Add the cooked tofu to the pan with BBQ sauce. Stir and coat evenly; simmer 2-3 minutes so it absorbs the flavor.
Garnish with sesame seeds or spring onion if desired. Serve with steamed rice, roasted veggies, or in wraps/bowls.
